title: Create model-internal tensors on the NPU input device

meta.symptom: PPDocLayoutV3 layout inference fails on NPU with a device mismatch when model post-processing creates fallback or sentinel tensors on the default device while masks, boxes, logits, or reference tensors are on npu or npu:<id>.
meta.root_cause: Bare torch tensor factories inside model forward or post-processing paths do not automatically follow the NPU device of nearby tensors. In the source run, mask_to_box_coordinate used fallback torch.tensor(torch.finfo(dtype).max) values without device placement, then combined them with mask-derived NPU tensors.
meta.trigger_fingerprint: NPU inference fails in model forward or post-processing near mask, box, index, coordinate, fallback, sentinel, or normalization tensor creation; code contains torch.tensor, torch.arange, torch.zeros, torch.ones, torch.full, or torch.empty calls without device=; nearby tensors are on npu or npu:<id>.
application_notes: Apply the rule only to tensor factories whose result is combined with an existing tensor or controls model output on an NPU path.
Use device=<reference_tensor>.device, such as device=mask.device for mask-derived post-processing tensors.

Add dtype=<target_dtype> when the factory creates numeric sentinels or normalizers expected to participate in arithmetic with tensors of that dtype; do not blindly use mask.dtype when the mask is boolean.
For model serving paths, also verify the config accepts npu and npu:<id> device strings and the local model is loaded with model.to(npu:<id>) rather than cuda:<id>.
validated_patterns: torch.tensor([width, height, width, height], device=mask.device, dtype=dtype)
verification: phase_fix_code reported that the NPU layout model device mismatch was fixed by creating mask_to_box_coordinate fallback tensors on the NPU device.
Direct PPDocLayoutDetector validation on npu:0 completed for examples/source/code.png with 1 page and 6 detected regions.
py_compile passed for the changed Python files, including PPDocLayoutV3 modeling code.
Final Phase 5 command exited 0; fresh serving_final_gate.json reported FULL_PASS, torch_npu_npu_available=True, cuda_fallback_detected=False, and cpu_fallback_detected=False.
antipatterns: Do not fix the mismatch by moving masks, boxes, logits, or model outputs back to CPU.
Do not leave sentinel factories such as torch.tensor(torch.finfo(dtype).max) without device placement in NPU post-processing code.
Do not use CUDA device strings for the local layout model after torch-npu migration when NPU execution is required.

trigger_fingerprint: NPU inference fails with device mismatch in model forward or post-processing while CUDA/CPU paths appear to work; stack trace points near fallback, sentinel, mask, box, index, or coordinate tensor creation; code contains bare torch tensor factories without device=input.device or dtype=input.dtype; model inputs, masks, logits, or boxes are on npu or npu:<id> but newly created tensors default to CPU.

classifier.reasoning: The reusable lesson is primarily a mechanical CUDA-to-NPU code transformation: tensor factories inside model forward or post-processing paths that derive values from existing tensors must create tensors on the same device, and often matching dtype, instead of defaulting to CPU. The surrounding validation steps are useful context, but the core reusable artifact is a deterministic code adaptation rule for torch.tensor, torch.arange, torch.ones, torch.zeros, and similar constructors near NPU tensor operations.
classifier.trigger_fingerprint: NPU inference fails with device mismatch in model forward or post-processing while CUDA/CPU paths appear to work; stack trace points near fallback, sentinel, mask, box, index, or coordinate tensor creation; code contains bare torch tensor factories without device=input.device or dtype=input.dtype; model inputs, masks, logits, or boxes are on npu or npu:<id> but newly created tensors default to CPU.

problem_description: PPDocLayoutV3 layout inference hit an NPU device mismatch because mask_to_box_coordinate created fallback/sentinel tensors without consistently placing them on the same device as the mask. Such hidden tensor factories are common in model post-processing and can silently work on CUDA/CPU while failing on NPU.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Locate tensor factories inside model forward or post-processing functions that derive values from NPU tensors. 2. Replace bare torch.tensor/torch.arange-style constructors with device=input.device and dtype=input.dtype where applicable. 3. Ensure project config accepts npu and npu:<id> device strings, and load the local layout model on npu:<id>. 4. Verify the detector directly on npu:0 and then rerun the full serving validation.
